file_name: BOLIVARMININGCORP_05_23_2003-EX-2.1-VISP WEB SITE BUILDING AND HOSTING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: Provider represents that these fees normally are charged to the customer according to the following schedule: $299.00 one time start-up and developmental fee due at contract signing $29.95 monthly maintenance and service fee 24/7 "800" Number Customer Service / Tech Support $25.00 per month for private labeled service and Free for the first 90 days for generic service. If the ISP does not have 250 users within the first 90 days we will charge $25.00 each month thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: Provider represents that these fees are variable and subject to change based on market conditions. Contact Provider for current fee schedule.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific fee schedule, the contract introduces uncertainty about the actual costs to KCI's customers. This omission is an in-text contradiction in the subsequent paragraph where the services are advertised to KCI customers as being available to KCI's Customers will receive an initial VISP web site setup including 5Mbs of storage space, maintenance of the site, technical support, and any services necessary for use of the site for no charge, potentially leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: KCI's Customers will receive an initial VISP web site setup including 5Mbs of storage space, maintenance of the site, technical support, and any services necessary for use of the site for no charge.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified fixed fees for the service. The changed text makes the fees variable and subject to change, directly contradicting the 'Contradicted Text,' which promises 'no charge' for the service. This omission of specific fees creates uncertainty and directly clashes with the promise of free service, rendering the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable regarding pricing.

perturbation.original_text: KCI pledges that it will not circumvent the relationships among venders, providers and clients developed by Provider either directly or indirectly, during the contract period and for a period of up to 2 (two) years following termination of this contract
perturbation.changed_text: KCI pledges to act in good faith with respect to existing vendor, provider, and client relationships.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the specific non-circumvention clause and the 2-year period following termination creates ambiguity. The phrase "act in good faith" is subjective and less enforceable than the original clause. This is an in-text contradiction in section 8 where there is lack of clarity, regarding assignments or transfers which could create conflicts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ASSIGNMENT This Agreement shall be binding upon the parties and their respective successors and assigns; provided, however, that no rights or obligations hereunder, including but not limited to Licensee's Subscriber accounts, shall be assigned or transferred, in whole or in part, by either of the parties hereto to any person, firm or corporation without prior written consent by the other party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may assign this Agreement, without the prior consent of the other party, to any person, partnership, firm or corporation affiliated by common ownership with the assigning party, acquiring all or substantially all of such party's assets or, in the case of PROVIDER, acquiring any assets of PROVIDER associated with its wholesale Internet access and services business.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a specific non-circumvention clause for a defined period (2 years), whereas the changed text introduces a subjective standard of 'good faith.' This contradicts the 'Assignment' section by creating ambiguity regarding the transfer of vendor/client relationships. The original clause offered clear protection, but the changed clause's lack of specificity potentially allows for assignments or transfers that could circumvent those relationships, which the original language sought to prevent, therefore creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event the Company fails to have its attorney issue the required opinion letter within 20 days of a written request from YourNetPlus.com or its nominee, the Company shall be liable for liquidated damages in the amount of 10% interest per 30 days on the value of the shares based on the closing bid price of the Company's common stock on the 20th business day following the date it receives written notice from YourNetPlus.com or their nominee. The damages shall accrue until the transfer agent receives the opinion letter
perturbation.changed_text: In the event the Company fails to have its attorney issue the required opinion letter, the Company shall be liable for damages.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific details of the liquidated damages calculation (10% interest per 30 days, etc.), the contract now lacks a clear method for determining the amount of damages. The text references liquidated damages, but lacks the specific calculation, the absence of a clear calculation mechanism creates ambiguity, making it hard to enforce which contradicts section 11, where it references regarding the validity, interpretation, performance and enforcement of the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONTROLLING LAW AND ARBITRATION All questions regarding the validity, interpretation, performance and enforcement of the provisions of this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the state of New York. Any controversy or claim arising out of, relating to or in connection with this Agreement that has not been resolved through the informal mediation of the parties shall be resolved through arbitration pursuant to the Commercial Arbitration Rules of the American Arbitration Association then in effect, as modified by the terms set forth below: (a) the arbitration shall be conducted in New York state; (b) the arbitration shall be conducted by a single arbitrator selected by the parties; (c) the parties shall act in a commercially reasonable manner and speedily select and then conduct the arbitration within 45 days with the expenditure of minimal discovery efforts and expense which shall be determined, if necessary, by the arbitrator. The agreement to arbitrate shall be specifically enforceable under prevailing New York law. Any award rendered by the arbitrator shall be binding and enforceable by any party to the arbitration and judgment shall be rendered upon it in a court of competent subject matter jurisdiction located in New York.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the specific calculation for liquidated damages, creating ambiguity regarding enforcement. The original text specifies '10% interest per 30 days', whereas the changed text merely states 'liable for damages'. This lack of specificity directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires 'validity, interpretation, performance and enforcement' of the agreement's provisions. The ambiguity in the changed text makes it difficult to interpret and enforce the damages provision, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
